title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Abuse, and Sensitive Business Flows · Go Semantics Case: Webhook Replay Ordering
summary: A webhook parses JSON before verifying raw bytes, accepts replay forever, fetches callback URLs from users, retries non-idempotent work, and rate-limits requests but not expensive business actions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo webhook replay ordering increment in webhook authenticity, replay, and abuse defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate timestamp or nonce window, event ID uniqueness, tenant, type, ordering, duplicate delivery, and durable processing state before effects.
Bound and retain exact raw body bytes, select expected signature scheme and key, compare in constant time, and only then parse and authorize the event.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Webhook Replay Ordering · Theory Webhook Replay Ordering
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9651 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine raw-message webhook authentication, replay bounds, and SSRF egress policy; then lower the byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at the owning boundary. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
